briefdescription.para: Call is active and ongoing.
Call is on hold.
Outgoing call is being dialed.
Outgoing call is being alerted (ringing on remote end)
Incoming call has arrived.
Incoming call is waiting (call waiting scenario)
Call held by Response and Hold feature.
The status of the call.

detaileddescription.para: Enumeration defining the various states a call can be in during HFP communication between HF and AG.

briefdescription.para: It is an outgoing call initiated by HF.
It is an incoming call from remote party.
The direction of the call.

detaileddescription.para: Enumeration defining whether the call was initiated by the HF (outgoing) or by the remote party (incoming).

briefdescription.para: Voice call.
Data call.
Fax transmission.
The mode of the call.

detaileddescription.para: Enumeration defining the type of call being established, whether voice, data, or fax transmission.

briefdescription.para: Register HFP HF profile.

detaileddescription.para: Register Handsfree profile callbacks to monitor the state and get the required HFP details to display.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Initiate the service level connection establishment procedure.

detaileddescription.para: Initiate the service level connection establishment procedure on the ACL connection specified by the parameter conn using the specific RFCOMM channel discovered by the function bt_br_discovery_start.

The parameter hf is a output parameter. When the service level connection establishment procedure is initiated without any error, the HFP HF object is allocated and it will be returned via the parameter hf if the parameter hf is not a NULL pointer.

When service level conenction is established, the registered callback connected will be triggered to notify the application that the service level connection establishment procedure is done. And the HFP HF object is valid at this time. It means after the function is called without any error, all interfaces provided by HFP HF can only be called after the registered callback connected is triggered.

parameterdescription.para: Peer RFCOMM channel to be connected.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Release the service level connection.

detaileddescription.para: Release the service level connection from the peer device.
The function can only be called after the registered callback connected is triggered.
If the function is called without any error, the HFP HF object is invalid at this time. All interfaces provided by HFP HF should not be called anymore.

If the service level connection is released, the registered callback disconnected will be triggered to notify the application that the service level connection release procedure is done. And the HFP HF object will be freed after the registered callback disconnected returned.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF enable/disable Calling Line Identification (CLI) Notification.

detaileddescription.para: Enable/disable Calling Line Identification (CLI) Notification. The AT command AT+CLIP will be sent to the AG to enable/disable the CLI unsolicited result code +CLIP when calling the function. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_CLI is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF report Gain of Microphone (VGM)

detaileddescription.para: Report Gain of Microphone (VGM). The AT command AT+VGM=<gain> will be sent to the AG to report its current microphone gain level setting to the AG. <gain> is a decimal numeric constant, relating to a particular (implementation dependent) volume level controlled by the HF. This command does not change the microphone gain of the AG; it simply indicates the current value of the microphone gain in the HF. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_VOLUME is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called. For "Volume Level Synchronization", the HF application could call the function to set VGM gain value in HF connection callback function. Then after the HF connection callback returned, VGM gain will be sent to HFP AG.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF report Gain of Speaker (VGS)

detaileddescription.para: Report Gain of Speaker (VGS). The AT command AT+VGS=<gain> will be sent to the AG to report its current speaker gain level setting to the AG. <gain> is a decimal numeric constant, relating to a particular (implementation dependent) volume level controlled by the HF. This command does not change the speaker gain of the AG; it simply indicates the current value of the speaker gain in the HF. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_VOLUME is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called. For "Volume Level Synchronization", the HF application could call the function to set VGS gain value in HF connection callback function. Then after the HF connection callback returned, VGS gain will be sent to HFP AG.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF requests currently selected operator.

detaileddescription.para: Send the AT+COPS? (Read) command to find the currently selected operator.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF accept the incoming call.

detaileddescription.para: Send the ATA command to accept the incoming call. OR, send the AT+BTRH=1 command to accept a held incoming call.

simplesect.para: It cannot be used when multiple calls are ongoing.
0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF reject the incoming call.

detaileddescription.para: Send the AT+CHUP command to reject the incoming call. OR, send the AT+BTRH=2 command to reject a held incoming call.

simplesect.para: It cannot be used when multiple calls are ongoing.
0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF terminate the incoming call.

detaileddescription.para: Send the AT+CHUP command to terminate the incoming call.

simplesect.para: It cannot be used when multiple calls are ongoing.
0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF put the incoming call on hold.

detaileddescription.para: Send the AT+BTRH=0 command to put the incoming call on hold. If the incoming call has been held, the callback on_hold will be triggered.

simplesect.para: It cannot be used when multiple calls are ongoing.
0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF query respond and hold status of AG.

detaileddescription.para: Send the AT+BTRH? command to query respond and hold status of AG. The status respond and hold will be notified through callback on_hold.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF phone number call.

detaileddescription.para: Initiate outgoing voice calls by providing the destination phone number to the AG. Send the ATDdd...dd; command to start phone number call. The result of the command will be notified through the callback dialing.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF memory dialing call.

detaileddescription.para: Initiate outgoing voice calls using the memory dialing feature of the AG. Send the ATD>nnn...; command to start memory dialing. The result of the command will be notified through the callback dialing.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF redial last number.

detaileddescription.para: Initiate outgoing voice calls by recalling the last number dialed by the AG. Send the AT+BLDN command to recall the last number. The result of the command will be notified through the callback dialing.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF setup audio connection.

detaileddescription.para: Setup audio conenction by sending AT+BCC if the Codec Negotiation is supported by both side. Or, initialize the SCO audio connection directly.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF set selected codec id.

detaileddescription.para: Set selected codec id by sending AT+BCS. The function is used to response the codec negotiation request notified by callback codec_negotiate. The parameter codec_id should be same as id of callback codec_negotiate if the id could be supported. Or, call bt_hfp_hf_set_codecs to notify the AG Codec IDs supported by HFP HF. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_CODEC_NEG is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF set supported codec ids.

detaileddescription.para: Set supported codec ids by sending AT+BAC. This function is used to notify AG the supported Codec IDs of HF. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_CODEC_NEG is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF turns off AG's EC and NR.

detaileddescription.para: Turn off the AG's EC and NR by sending AT+NREC=0. The result of the command is notified through the callback ecnr_turn_off. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_ECNR is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF enable/disable call waiting notification.

detaileddescription.para: Enable call waiting notification by sending AT+CCWA=1. Disable call waiting notification by sending AT+CCWA=0. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_3WAY_CALL is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF release all held calls.

detaileddescription.para: Release all held calls by sending AT+CHLD=0. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_3WAY_CALL is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF set User Determined User Busy (UDUB) for a waiting call.

detaileddescription.para: Set User Determined User Busy (UDUB) for a waiting call by sending AT+CHLD=0. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_3WAY_CALL is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF release all active calls and accept other call.

detaileddescription.para: Release all active calls (if any exist) and accepts the other (held or waiting) call by sending AT+CHLD=1. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_3WAY_CALL is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF hold all active calls and accept other call.

detaileddescription.para: Hold all active calls (if any exist) and accepts the other (held or waiting) call by sending AT+CHLD=2. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_3WAY_CALL is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF add a held call to the conversation.

detaileddescription.para: Add a held call to the conversation by sending AT+CHLD=3. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_3WAY_CALL is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF explicit call transfer.

detaileddescription.para: Connects the two calls and disconnects the subscriber from both calls (Explicit Call Transfer) by sending AT+CHLD=4. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_3WAY_CALL is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF release call with specified index.

detaileddescription.para: Release call with specified index by sending AT+CHLD=1<idx>. <idx> is index of specified call. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_ECC is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF request private consultation mode with specified call.

detaileddescription.para: Request private consultation mode with specified call (Place all calls on hold EXCEPT the call indicated by <idx>.) by sending AT+CHLD=2<idx>. <idx> is index of specified call. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_ECC is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF enable/disable the voice recognition function.

detaileddescription.para: Enables/disables the voice recognition function in the AG. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_VOICE_RECG is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF indicate that the HF is ready to accept audio.

detaileddescription.para: This value indicates that the HF is ready to accept audio when the Audio Connection is first established. The HF shall only send this value if the eSCO link has been established. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_ENH_VOICE_RECG is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF attach a phone number for a voice tag.

detaileddescription.para: Send AT command "AT+BINP=1" to request phone number to the AG.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF Transmit A specific DTMF Code.

detaileddescription.para: During an ongoing call, the HF transmits the AT+VTS command to instruct the AG to transmit a specific DTMF code to its network connection. The set of the code is "0-9,#,*,A-D".

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF Query Subscriber Number Information.

detaileddescription.para: It allows HF to query the AG subscriber number by sending AT+CNUM.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF set AG indicator activated/deactivated status.

detaileddescription.para: It allows HF to issue the AT+BIA command if it needs to change the activated/deactivated status of indicators in the AG. The index of all indicators can be activated/deactivated are defined in enum hfp_hf_ag_indicators. The each bit of parameter status represents the indicator status corresponding to the index. Such as, value 0b111110 of status means the AG indicator service is required to be deactivated. Others are required to be activated.

parameterdescription.para: The activated/deactivated bitmap status of AG indicators.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF enable/disable enhanced safety.

detaileddescription.para: It allows HF to transfer of HF indicator enhanced safety value. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_HF_INDICATOR_ENH_SAFETY is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF remaining battery level.

detaileddescription.para: It allows HF to transfer of HF indicator remaining battery level value. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_HF_INDICATOR_BATTERY is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Handsfree HF query list of current calls.

detaileddescription.para: It allows HF to query list of current calls by sending AT+CLCC command. If CONFIG_BT_HFP_HF_ECS is not enabled, the error -ENOTSUP will be returned if the function called.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Hands Free Profile (HFP)
